expectations.text: Flags that calling GetQueryableAsync (or composing IQueryable / LINQ) inside the AppService is wrong
expectations.evidence: Custom query in the application layer ... Calling GetQueryableAsync() from the app service leaks EF Core composition (LINQ provider semantics, tracking behavior) into a layer that, per the dependency table, must not know about EF Core.

expectations.text: Recommends defining a custom repository interface (e.g. IOrderRepository : IRepository<Order, Guid>) in the Domain project
expectations.evidence: File: src/Acme.MyProject.Domain/Orders/IOrderRepository.cs ... public interface IOrderRepository : IRepository<Order, Guid>

expectations.text: Shows the EF Core implementation of that custom repository (e.g. inheriting EfCoreRepository<...> in the EntityFrameworkCore project) where the IQueryable / LINQ logic actually lives
expectations.evidence: File: src/Acme.MyProject.EntityFrameworkCore/Orders/EfCoreOrderRepository.cs ... public class EfCoreOrderRepository : EfCoreRepository<MyProjectDbContext, Order, Guid>, IOrderRepository

expectations.text: Refactored AppService injects the custom repository and calls a method that returns materialised results (List<Order> / PagedResultDto / count + list), with no GetQueryableAsync call remaining
expectations.evidence: private readonly IOrderRepository _orderRepository; ... var totalCount = await _orderRepository.GetActiveCountForCustomerAsync(...); var orders = await _orderRepository.GetActiveListForCustomerAsync(...); No IQueryable, no AsyncExecuter, no GetQueryableAsync in the app service.

expectations.text: Registers the custom repository through ABP's repository pipeline — e.g. options.AddRepository<Order, OrderRepository>() inside the EF Core module's AddAbpDbContext<>() configuration, or via the auto-registration that follows from inheriting EfCoreRepository<,,> in the same EF Core project. Does NOT register via generic services.AddScoped<IOrderRepository, OrderRepository>() / IServiceCollection extension methods (an ABP-specific DI lock-in)
expectations.evidence: ABP auto-registers any class that inherits EfCoreRepository<,,> and implements a custom interface derived from IRepository<,>, so the custom repo is picked up by convention once AddDefaultRepositories() is called. ... options.AddDefaultRepositories(); options.AddRepository<Order, EfCoreOrderRepository>();
